risks.title: Affordability Challenges
risks.reasoning: The economic mechanism at play is limited housing affordability despite increased supply due to rapid population growth. Data from cities like Vancouver, Montreal, and Halifax show high demand and limited rental vacancies. Renters aged 25-34 and low-income owners are most exposed due to stagnant incomes and high cost of living. We are confident in this assessment as 39 diverse agent groups flagged it.
risks.affected_groups: 25-34 renters, low-income owners across multiple cities
risks.cascade_effect: The combination of high rents, population growth, and limited affordable options creates financial strain and housing insecurity.

risks.title: Displacement due to Rising Property Values
risks.reasoning: New housing supply can inadvertently raise property values, leading to displacement of lower-income residents. Data from cities like Vancouver and Kelowna reveal rapidly increasing home values. Senior owners 65+ and renters in rural areas are particularly vulnerable. This risk is well-supported as it was identified by 4 independent agents.
risks.cascade_effect: Higher rents could force demographic relocation further from support networks.

risks.title: Infrastructure Strain
risks.reasoning: Rapid development without parallel infrastructure expansion can reduce quality of life. Cities like Ottawa and Toronto may face overcrowding. Homeowners aged 35-49 with high investment in local areas are highly affected. We have medium confidence based on 2 agent reports corroborating this.
risks.cascade_effect: Strained infrastructure could lead to increased commute times and impact employment.

risk_report.blind_spots: The impact on single-parent families has not been extensively analyzed.
risk_report.key_insight: Affordability challenges are interconnected with infrastructure strains and displacement risks, making them difficult to address in isolation.

confidence.reason: strong StatsCan data coverage for this policy type; national scope matches agent distribution well; all agents responded successfully
confidence.caveat: This is a hypothesis generation tool. Findings should be validated against real survey data before informing policy decisions.
